mappings.rationale: Directly covers the history of agriculture, farm management, crop farming, and livestock farming that form the core of this section.
Covers food production, processing, preservation, and quality control, all major topics in this section.
Relevant to food quality evaluation, nutritional aspects of food sources, and new product development.
Relevant to crop farming including plant propagation, seeding and cultivation, and specialized crop techniques.
Relevant to livestock farming, animal breeding, and the management of flock and stock animals.
Relevant to pest and disease control, farming in relation to pollution, and sustainable agricultural practices.
Directly relevant to forest crops including trees and rubber, listed under specialized crop farming.
Relevant to fermentation processes in food preservation and beverage production, and to disease control in livestock.
Relevant to control of pests and disease organisms in crop farming and disease control in livestock.
Relevant to fermentation, food preservation, food quality control, and disease organisms affecting crops and livestock.
Relevant to crop disease organisms, fermentation in food processing, and yeast-based food and beverage production.
Relevant to disease control in livestock farming and food-borne illness prevention in food quality regulation.
Relevant to pest control in crop farming and beekeeping as a form of livestock farming.
Relevant to animal breeding and livestock management, and to the regulation of food quality regarding hormones in food production.
Relevant to disease and pest control in livestock farming and food safety.
Relevant to sustainable agriculture, environmental impact of farming, and new food source development.
Relevant to farming in relation to weather and the environmental challenges facing agricultural technology.
Relevant to animal breeding, plant propagation, and the genetic basis of crop and livestock improvement.
Relevant to modern agricultural biotechnology for crop improvement and animal breeding.
Relevant to the geographic distribution of crops and livestock, including tropical and dryland farming.
Relevant to the biology of major livestock animals including cattle, swine, sheep, goats, and horses.
Covers the economic history of agriculture and its transformation through industrialization.
Relevant to animal breeding and plant propagation through selective breeding and hereditary principles.
Relevant to the control of pest organisms and disease in crop farming.
Relevant to dryland farming techniques as a specialized form of crop farming.
